whyExists: Database schemas evolve over time — adding new columns, modifying table structures, or adding indexes. Changing production database tables manually causes data loss and environment drift. Alembic is the official database migration tool for SQLAlchemy, providing version control for your database schema.
curiosityQuestion: How does Alembic know which migration scripts have already been applied to your database?
problemItSolves: Automates database schema changes safely without destroying existing production data.
realWorldAnalogy: Git for your database schema: each migration file is like a commit that records specific schema changes (`upgrade()`) and how to undo them (`downgrade()`).
simpleExplanation:
Commands:
- Initialize: `alembic init alembic`
- Generate migration: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add age'`
- Apply migration: `alembic upgrade head`
- Rollback: `alembic downgrade -1`

examples.title: Alembic Upgrade & Downgrade Functions

visualDiagram:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    v1["Revision 1: initial_db"] --> v2["Revision 2: add_user_age"]
    v2 --> v3["Revision 3: add_indexes (head)"]
```

stepByStepExecution.explanation: Reads alembic_version table, applies all unapplied migration scripts in sequence up to head.

memoryDiagram.stack: Database tracks applied revisions in alembic_version table

commonMistakes: Modifying an existing migration file after it has already been applied in production! Always create a new revision instead.
